Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students examine the muscular and skeletal systems of the human body and how the muscles and bones work together to produce movement.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
Elaboration: Through practical activities, they explore, from a biophysical perspective, the major components of the musculoskeletal system and its contributions and interactions during physical activity, sport and exercise.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
ContentDescription: Possible causes of illness and injury to the musculoskeletal system are investigated.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
Elaboration: Strategies and aids to assist in the prevention and management of such conditions are also explored.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
Elaboration: Students consider a variety of permitted and prohibited substances and methods used to enhance performance of the musculoskeletal system.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should participate in and analyse information from a variety of practical activities to explain how the muscular and skeletal systems function and interact to produce movement, and evaluate the use of performance enhancement substances and methods.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How does the musculoskeletal system work to produce movement?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students investigate the cardiovascular and respiratory systems of the human body and how the heart, blood vessels and lungs function at rest and during physical activity.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
Elaboration: Through practical activities, students explore the structures and function of the cardiorespiratory system and the contributions and interactions of each system during physical activity, sport and exercise at various intensities.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
Elaboration: The impacts of regular aerobic exercise on the functioning of these systems are also examined.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
Elaboration: Students consider a variety of permitted and prohibited substances and methods used to enhance performance of the cardiorespiratory system.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
Elaboration: They also explore the ethical and sociocultural considerations of using permitted and prohibited performance-enhancing substances and methods.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to participate in and analyse information from a variety of practical activities to explain how the cardiovascular and respiratory systems function and interact, and evaluate the use of performance enhancement substances and methods.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What role does the cardiorespiratory system play in movement?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students focus on the role of physical activity, sport and exercise in developing and promoting healthy lifestyles across the lifespan.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
Elaboration: Students explore the sociocultural influences on participation in various forms of physical activity.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
Elaboration: They investigate the physical, social, mental, emotional and spiritual benefits of participation in regular physical activity at the individual and population levels, and the potential health risks associated with physical inactivity and sedentary behaviour.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
ContentDescription: Students examine sociocultural factors that influence physical activity and consider opportunities and barriers to participation.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
Elaboration: They develop an understanding of the use of subjective and objective methods for assessing physical activity and sedentary behaviour at the individual and population levels and compare these to physical activity and sedentary behaviour guidelines.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
Elaboration: Students identify and describe the components of the social-ecological model to assist in the critique and creation of strategies aimed at increasing physical activity and/or reducing sedentary behaviour within a given population.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
Elaboration: Students conduct a Functional Movement Assessment (FMA), then design and implement a personalised plan that is sustainable and adheres to the physical activity and sedentary behaviour guidelines.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to collect and analyse data related to individual and population levels of participation in physical activity and sedentary behaviour and conduct an FMA to create, undertake and evaluate a personalised plan that promotes adherence to the relevant physical activity and sedentary behaviour guidelines.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How do physical activity, sport and exercise contribute to healthy lifestyles?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students focus on a range of contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport at the local, national and global levels.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
ContentDescription: They investigate a range of intrapersonal and interpersonal factors that affect access to, and inclusion, participation and performance in, physical activity and sport, such as injuries, coaching, sports technology and the media, psychological strategies and equity for a range of population groups, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Peoples.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
ContentDescription: Students explore one contemporary issue relevant to physical activity and/or sport and prescribe and participate in practical activities to highlight the issue.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
ContentDescription: Students develop an understanding of the historical and current perspectives on the issue and forecast future trends.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
Elaboration: They form conclusions about the impacts these issues have on physical activity and sport in society.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to explain a range of intrapersonal and interpersonal contemporary issues that influence access to, and inclusion, participation and performance in, physical activity and sport at the local, national and global levels.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Physical activity, sport, exercise and society
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - What are the contemporary issues associated with physical activity and sport?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How are movement skills improved?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students examine the biomechanical and skill-acquisition principles that can be applied when analysing and improving movement skills for participation and performance.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How are movement skills improved?
ContentDescription: Through practical activities, students explore and analyse their own movement and use coaching to investigate factors that influence skill acquisition.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How are movement skills improved?
Elaboration: They develop an understanding of how appropriately applying biomechanical and skill-acquisition principles leads to the development of optimal movement patterns to enhance participation and performance.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How are movement skills improved?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to analyse primary data collected from participation in physical activity, sport and exercise to develop and refine movement skills from an individual and coaching perspective, by applying biomechanical and skill-acquisition principles.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - How are movement skills improved?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students explore the various systems and mechanisms associated with the production of energy required for human movement.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
Elaboration: They consider the cardiovascular, respiratory and muscular systems and the roles of each in supplying oxygen to, and creating energy at, the working muscles.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
Elaboration: They examine the ways in which energy for movement is produced by the 3 energy systems and the associated fuels used for physical activity, sport and exercise of varying intensity and duration.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
Elaboration: Students also consider the many factors contributing to fatigue, nutritional tools to delay fatigue and recovery strategies used to optimise the return to pre-exercise conditions.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
Elaboration: Through practical activities, students explore the interplay of the energy systems during physical activity, sport and exercise.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to use data collected in practical activities to analyse how the major body and energy systems work together to enable movements to occur; explain the factors causing fatigue; and recommend suitable recovery strategies.

Subject: PE
UnitDescription: Movement skills and energy for physical activity, sport and exercise
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How does the body produce energy?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 2.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - What are the foundations of an effective training program?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students analyse the information required to form the foundation of an effective training program.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - What are the foundations of an effective training program?
Elaboration: Through participation, they undertake and collect data from an activity analysis and justify the specific physiological requirements of an activity.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - What are the foundations of an effective training program?
ContentDescription: Students determine the relevant factors that affect each of the fitness components and conduct an assessment of fitness that demonstrates correct and appropriate implementation of testing protocols and procedures and informs the design of the training program.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - What are the foundations of an effective training program?
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to undertake an activity analysis to justify the physiological requirements of an activity that informs an appropriate assessment of fitness.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 1 - What are the foundations of an effective training program?
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 1.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How is training implemented effectively to improve fitness?
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students focus on participation, implementation and evaluation of training principles and methods from practical and theoretical perspectives.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How is training implemented effectively to improve fitness?
Elaboration: They consider the ways in which fitness can be improved by applying appropriate training principles and methods when designing and critiquing a training program.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How is training implemented effectively to improve fitness?
Elaboration: Students identify and consider components of an exercise training session, and they record and analyse relevant data that can be used to adjust training.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 2 - How is training implemented effectively to improve fitness?
Elaboration: Students explain the chronic adaptations of the cardiovascular, respiratory and muscular systems that improve fitness and enhance performance.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
ContentDescription: In this area of study, students reflect on their participation in a practical activity and use primary data collected to demonstrate their integration of theory and practice across Units 3 and 4.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
Elaboration: Using an interdisciplinary approach, students are required to analyse the interrelationships between skill acquisition, biomechanics, energy production and training, and the impacts these have on performance.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
ContentDescription: To do this, students reflect on their participation in either:

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
ContentDescription: The specific practical activity can occur at any time during Unit 3 or Unit 4, as evidenced in students’ reflective folio.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
ContentDescription: An extended response will be used to assess this area of study, in which students will answer a prompt(s) utilising the evidence recorded in their reflective folio.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
Elaboration: Prior to the extended response, students will use a planning tool such as a mind map to determine and explore links between theoretical concepts and the practical activity.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
ContentDescription: On completion of this unit, the student should be able to integrate theory and practice that enables them to analyse the interrelationships between skill acquisition, biomechanics, energy production and training, and the impacts these have on performance.

Subject: PE
AreaofStudy: Area of Study 3 - Integrated movement experiences
ContentDescription: To achieve this outcome, the student will draw on key knowledge and key skills outlined in Area of Study 3.
